Defense Compliance

CMMC Compliance
Consulting

CMMC 2.0 certification is now a contract requirement across the defense industrial base. Our practice guides defense contractors through readiness assessment, gap remediation, and pre-assessment preparation — ensuring successful certification the first time.

From Gap Analysis to Certification

The Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) 2.0 framework requires defense contractors to demonstrate verifiable cybersecurity practices before winning contracts. For many organizations — particularly small and mid-size subcontractors — the requirements represent a significant operational and technical challenge.

Our team has operated within the defense industrial base at Raytheon and across Fortune 50 defense programs. We understand both the technical controls required and the operational realities of implementing them. Our engagements are structured to achieve certification efficiently, without disrupting core business operations.

CMMC Engagement Process

  • CMMC Level 2 and Level 3 readiness assessment against all 110 NIST 800-171 controls
  • Gap analysis with prioritized remediation roadmap and cost estimates
  • CUI scope definition and data flow documentation
  • System Security Plan (SSP) development and documentation
  • Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) creation and management
  • Technical control implementation guidance and validation
  • Policy and procedure development aligned to CMMC requirements
  • Pre-assessment mock evaluation with a CMMC-AB aligned methodology
  • C3PAO assessment preparation and readiness confirmation
  • Continuous monitoring program implementation for ongoing compliance

Why CMMC Matters Now

The Department of Defense is actively enforcing CMMC requirements in contract solicitations. Defense contractors that cannot demonstrate compliance risk exclusion from current and future contracts. The ramp-up period for achieving certification typically requires 6-12 months for organizations starting from baseline — making immediate action essential for contract eligibility.

Who We Serve

Defense prime contractors preparing for CMMC Level 2 or Level 3 assessment. Small and mid-size subcontractors handling CUI for the first time. Organizations that received a negative DIBCAC assessment. Managed service providers serving the defense industrial base who need to demonstrate CMMC compliance to their clients.

CMMC Services

  • Readiness Assessment
  • Gap Remediation
  • SSP & POA&M Development
  • Control Implementation
  • Mock Assessment
  • C3PAO Preparation
  • Continuous Monitoring

Defense Background

  • Raytheon Experience
  • Fortune 50 Defense Programs
  • ITAR-Cleared Operations
  • 26,000+ Industry Following
